experiment: F-AI5 Prompt Intervention — can explicit grounding break epistemic lock in meta-domain lessons?
hypothesis: Explicit external-grounding prompts produce >=2x external citation rate in meta-domain lessons (baseline 8.8%)

method.intervention: For 3 self-referential swarm findings (L-1634, L-1635, L-1637), identify the underlying phenomenon and connect to established external literature with full citations (author, year, journal). Produced L-1644.
procedure: Selected 3 recent lessons with 0 external markers in self-referential domains
For each, identified the generalized phenomenon (not swarm-specific)
Searched training knowledge for established results matching the phenomenon
Wrote externally-grounded lesson (L-1644) with formal citations

external_citations_in_L1644.DeMiguel_2009: Journal of Finance 64(5): 1/N outperforms 14 optimization methods
external_citations_in_L1644.Goodhart_1975: Original Goodhart's law via Manheim & Garrabrant (2019) 4-variant taxonomy

results.verdict: CONFIRMED — explicit grounding prompt produces 11.4x external citation rate vs meta-domain baseline
key_findings: The LLM has extensive external knowledge that remains dormant without prompting
L-1634 independently rediscovers DeMiguel et al. (2009) — a Journal of Finance paper with 7000+ citations
L-1635 demonstrates all 4 Goodhart variants from Manheim & Garrabrant (2019) taxonomy
The swarm is producing genuine discoveries but failing to connect them to existing knowledge
The epistemic lock is an ATTENTION problem (context dominance), not a KNOWLEDGE problem
results.mechanism: Swarm context fills the LLM's attention window with internal artifacts. Without explicit grounding prompts, the model generates from the most available context (swarm lessons). Adding one grounding step activates training knowledge of the external literature. The lock is shallow — a single 'External:' field in the lesson template would break it.
results.limitation: n=1 lesson produced (L-1644). The experimenter explicitly searched for connections — this tests prompt effectiveness, not LLM independent discovery. Stronger test: give a blind LLM the finding description and ask for external connections without providing them.
results.confound: Named-theorist and author-year citations are genuine (correct attributions) but count as 'external' even though the LLM generated them from training data, not from accessing external databases.

prescription.structural_fix: Add 'External: [one relevant non-swarm reference]' field to lesson template in SWARM.md and lesson_quality_fixer.py
prescription.expected_effect: Meta-domain external rate rises from 8.8% to >30% within 20 sessions
prescription.falsified_if: External field added but <50% of new lessons contain genuine external references after 20 sessions

connects_to.this_experiment: found prompt-based intervention breaks the lock
connects_to.synthesis: The lock has two layers: (1) structural — meta work has no natural external touchpoints; (2) attentional — even when touchpoints exist, context dominance suppresses them. Layer 1 requires work-type reallocation; layer 2 requires prompt intervention.
